name: auth
context: Supabase Auth — sign-up, sign-in, OAuth providers, magic links, MFA, and session management
info: Supabase Auth (powered by GoTrue) provides a complete authentication system: email/password sign-up and sign-in, 20+ OAuth providers (Google, GitHub, Apple, Discord, etc.), magic link (passwordless email), phone/SMS OTP, SAML SSO, and multi-factor authentication. Auth issues JWTs that the PostgREST API validates against Row Level Security policies. The `auth.users` table in Postgres stores all user records with metadata. The client SDK exposes `supabase.auth.signUp()`, `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`, `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth()`, and `supabase.auth.signInWithOtp()`. Sessions are automatically refreshed. Use `supabase.auth.getUser()` (not `getSession()`) for server-side verification as it validates the JWT against the auth server.

sections.title: Email/password authentication
sections.body: Use `supabase.auth.signUp()` with `email` and `password` for registration. Optionally pass `data` in `options` for custom user metadata. Use `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` for login. If email confirmation is enabled, the session is null until the user clicks the confirmation link. Handle the redirect with `exchangeCodeForSession()` in the callback route.

sections.title: OAuth providers
sections.body: Call `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' })` to redirect to the OAuth provider. Set `redirectTo` in options to your callback URL. Handle the callback by extracting the `code` parameter and calling `exchangeCodeForSession(code)`. Configure provider credentials in the dashboard or `config.toml`. The PKCE flow is used by default for security.

sections.title: Magic link / passwordless
sections.body: Call `supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ email })` to send a magic link. The user clicks the link and is redirected to `emailRedirectTo` with a code. Handle the callback the same way as OAuth. Magic links expire after 5 minutes by default. For phone OTP, use `signInWithOtp({ phone })` then `verifyOtp()` with the SMS code.

sections.title: Session management
sections.body: Sessions contain an `access_token` (JWT, 1 hour) and `refresh_token`. The client auto-refreshes tokens. Use `onAuthStateChange()` to react to auth events: `SIGNED_IN`, `SIGNED_OUT`, `TOKEN_REFRESHED`, `USER_UPDATED`, `PASSWORD_RECOVERY`. On the server, always use `getUser()` which validates the JWT. On the client, `getSession()` reads from local storage without validation.

links.context: RLS policies use auth.uid() and auth.jwt() from auth tokens
Client initialization and SSR setup for auth
Configure OAuth providers in config.toml for local dev
Edge functions can use service role client for admin auth operations
Next.js middleware for auth session refresh and route protection

related.note: Auth JWTs are the foundation for RLS policy evaluation
Client SDK must be initialized before using auth methods

scripts.name: Complete auth flow
scripts.description: Sign up, sign in, get user, and sign out with Supabase Auth

depends.reason: Auth methods are called on the Supabase client instance
